target.title: fix(telegram): explain disabled plugin approval failures
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves
Fixes #95800.

Telegram-originated plugin approvals, such as `skill_workshop apply`, could fail with a generic no-route or timeout message when Telegram native approvals were unavailable because `channels.telegram.execApprovals.approvers` and `commands.ownerAllowFrom` were not configured. The Telegram setup guidance already existed, but the agent-facing plugin approval failure did not surface it.

## Why This Change Was Made
The approval hook already receives the turn source. When a plugin approval fails with no route or times out, it now resolves the initiating plugin approval surface for that channel/account and appends setup text only when that same plugin surface is disabled and the channel explicitly provides plugin-approval setup guidance.

Telegram opts into that plugin setup guidance by reusing its existing native exec approval setup text, because Telegram plugin approvals are blocked by the same unconfigured approver gate reported in #95800. Split-routing channels that only expose exec setup text stay generic for plugin no-route/timeouts, so operators are not pointed at `approvals.exec` when the plugin route is the failing surface.

This keeps the current approver-gating model intact and does not auto-route or enable Telegram approvals.

## User Impact
Telegram-only operators get an actionable explanation telling them to use Web UI or terminal UI for now, or configure `channels.telegram.execApprovals.approvers` / `commands.ownerAllowFrom`, instead of a silent timeout-shaped denial.

## Evidence
- Current head: `34e3c10e23ec8d3cf00ff803188df4226cd60f8d`.
- Behavior proof, focused on the reported approval path and the split-route regression ClawSweeper requested: `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.e2e.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/approval-native.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/exec-approvals.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/plugin-approval.test.ts src/infra/plugin-approval-forwarder.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/approval-delivery-helpers.test.ts src/channels/plugins/approvals.test.ts` passed 6 Vitest shards, 182 tests.
- The agent approval coverage asserts Telegram-originated plugin no-route and timeout failures include:

```text
Approve it from the Web UI or terminal UI for now. Telegram supports native exec approvals for this account. Configure `channels.telegram.execApprovals.approvers` or `commands.ownerAllowFrom`; leave `channels.telegram.execApprovals.enabled` unset/`auto` or set it to `true`.
```

- Regression coverage also asserts split-route plugin no-route and plugin timeout failures stay generic when exec delivery is disabled and the channel has no plugin-specific setup guidance.
- `pnpm format:check -- src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.ts src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.e2e.test.ts src/infra/exec-approval-surface.ts src/channels/plugins/types.adapters.ts src/channels/plugins/approvals.ts src/channels/plugins/approvals.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/approval-delivery-helpers.ts src/plugin-sdk/approval-delivery-helpers.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/approval-native.ts extensions/telegram/src/approval-native.test.ts` passed.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Mantis Telegram Desktop Proof run `28051331964` attempted real Telegram Desktop proof on the previous head but did not capture usable visual evidence. Its manifest says the approval-failure chat path could not be reproduced in the available capture environment, so it produced no PR proof artifact.

## Notes
- The first push to `openclaw/openclaw` failed with GitHub 403, so this PR is from the `MonkeyLeeT/openclaw` fork with maintainer edits enabled.
- Issue assignment to `@steipete` also failed from this token with GitHub 403.
- Local Crabbox proof could not start from this machine because AWS credentials were unavailable: `failed to refresh cached credentials, no EC2 IMDS role found`.
target.author: monkeyleet
